description: Examples of wealth management metrics using DAX functions for advanced analytics

examples.name: YTD Portfolio Return (DAX)
examples.description: Year-to-date portfolio return using DAX time intelligence functions
examples.traditional_formula: SUM(CASE WHEN date_part('year', transaction_date) = date_part('year', CURRENT_DATE) THEN amount END)

examples.name: 12-Month Rolling Volatility (DAX)
examples.description: 12-month rolling volatility using DAX date functions
examples.traditional_formula: STDDEV(returns) OVER (ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 11 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)

examples.name: Top 5 Holdings by Value (DAX)
examples.description: Top 5 portfolio holdings using DAX ranking functions
examples.traditional_formula: SELECT * FROM portfolio WHERE market_value IN (SELECT market_value FROM portfolio ORDER BY market_value DESC LIMIT 5)
advantages: Easy to change number of items

examples.name: Sharpe Ratio Quartile (DAX)
examples.description: Sharpe ratio quartile ranking using DAX statistical functions
examples.dax_formula: SWITCH(TRUE(), [Sharpe Ratio] >= PERCENTILEX.INC('Portfolio', [Sharpe Ratio], 0.75), "Q4 (Top)", [Sharpe Ratio] >= PERCENTILEX.INC('Portfolio', [Sharpe Ratio], 0.5), "Q3", [Sharpe Ratio] >= PERCENTILEX.INC('Portfolio', [Sharpe Ratio], 0.25), "Q2", "Q1 (Bottom)")
examples.traditional_formula: CASE WHEN sharpe_ratio >= (SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY sharpe_ratio) FROM portfolio) THEN 'Q4 (Top)' ... END

examples.name: Benchmark Correlation (DAX)
examples.description: Portfolio correlation with benchmark using DAX statistical functions

examples.name: Conditional Risk Level (DAX)
examples.description: Risk level classification using DAX logical functions
examples.dax_formula: SWITCH(TRUE(), [Volatility] > 0.25, "High Risk", [Volatility] > 0.15, "Medium Risk", [Volatility] > 0.05, "Low Risk", "Very Low Risk")
examples.traditional_formula: CASE WHEN volatility > 0.25 THEN 'High Risk' WHEN volatility > 0.15 THEN 'Medium Risk' WHEN volatility > 0.05 THEN 'Low Risk' ELSE 'Very Low Risk' END

examples.name: Year-over-Year Growth Rate (DAX)
examples.description: YoY growth using DAX time intelligence
examples.dax_formula: ([Portfolio Value] - CALCULATE([Portfolio Value], SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))) / CALCULATE([Portfolio Value], SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
examples.traditional_formula: (current_value - LAG(current_value, 12) OVER (ORDER BY date)) / LAG(current_value, 12) OVER (ORDER BY date)

examples.name: Weighted Average Beta (DAX)
examples.description: Portfolio beta weighted by position size using DAX iterators

examples.name: Selected Benchmark Return (DAX)
examples.description: Dynamic benchmark return based on user selection using DAX information functions
examples.traditional_formula: CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT name) FROM benchmark WHERE active = true) = 1 THEN (SELECT return FROM benchmark WHERE active = true) ELSE NULL END

examples.name: YTD Upside Capture (DAX)
examples.description: Year-to-date upside capture ratio using DAX time intelligence and filtering
examples.dax_formula: AVERAGEX(FILTER('Portfolio', [Benchmark Return] > 0 && 'Calendar'[Date] >= DATEADD(TODAY(), -1, YEAR)), [Portfolio Return] / [Benchmark Return])
examples.traditional_formula: AVG(portfolio_return / benchmark_return) FROM portfolio WHERE benchmark_return > 0 AND date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())

implementation_notes.parser_integration: Extend semantic layer formula parser to recognize DAX syntax
implementation_notes.context_handling: Implement proper evaluation context for row, filter, and query contexts
implementation_notes.performance_optimization: Cache frequently used DAX calculations, especially time intelligence functions
implementation_notes.governance_workflow: DAX functions marked as 'draft' require steward review before production use
implementation_notes.testing_strategy: Unit tests for each DAX function with sample wealth management data
implementation_notes.documentation: Auto-generate function reference from DAX engine metadata
